title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Failure Case: Context Budget
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt context budget increment in a capability and surface decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Context Budget
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Context Budget contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value.

content.text: Allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Context Budget, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a capability and surface decision matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: More context always improves output quality. This fails the Context Budget contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Context Budget evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-context-budget-1a5haz" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Budget into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-context-budget-1a5haz
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Context Budget model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Context Budget applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value.
